#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    // Get the observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);

    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // 3. Add observer relationship (target observes the observer)
    graph.addRelationship(target, observer);

    // 4. Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // 5. Record call count before reset
    int initialCount = observer->getCallCount();

    // 6. Reset observer node
    observer->reset();

    // 7. Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // 8. Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != initialCount) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// Data model definitions
class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// Function implementation
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create source and observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("source");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: source -> observer
    graph.addObserver(source, observer);

    // Notify source and verify observer was notified
    source->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset observer state
    observer->valueChanged(false);

    // Close observer node
    observer->closeNode();

    // Notify source again
    source->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {

    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    auto recompute_count = reaction::var(0);
    
    // Create a calculation that depends on a and b, and increments the recompute count
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([a, b, &recompute_count]() {
        recompute_count = recompute_count + 1;
        return a() + b();
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a = nonBatchA;
    b = nonBatchB;
    
    int non_batch_count = recompute_count.get();
    
    // Perform batch updates
    auto batch = reaction::batch();
    a = batchA;
    b = batchB;
    batch.execute();
    
    int batch_count = recompute_count.get();
    
    return {sum_calc.get(), non_batch_count, batch_count};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <cmath>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive sensor variable
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Track recomputation count and last processed value
    auto recompute_count = reaction::var(0);
    auto last_processed = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create filtered sensor that only updates when change exceeds threshold
    auto filtered_sensor = reaction::filter(sensor, [diffThreshold](double current, double previous) {
        return std::abs(current - previous) > diffThreshold;
    });
    
    // Calculation that determines status and updates tracking variables
    auto status_calc = reaction::calc([filtered_sensor, last_processed, recompute_count, levelThreshold]() {
        double current_val = filtered_sensor.get();
        double prev_val = last_processed.get();
        
        // Only update if the change meets the threshold (should always be true due to filter)
        if (std::abs(current_val - prev_val) > diffThreshold) {
            recompute_count.set(recompute_count.get() + 1);
            last_processed.set(current_val);
        }
        
        return current_val > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    
    // Update sensor with first value
    sensor.set(firstUpdate);
    int count_after_first = recompute_count.get();
    
    // Update sensor with second value  
    sensor.set(secondUpdate);
    int count_after_second = recompute_count.get();
    
    // Get final status
    std::string final_status = status_calc.get();
    
    return std::make_tuple(final_status, count_after_first, count_after_second);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: reactiveVar + increment
    auto reactiveExpr = reactiveVar + increment;
    
    // Create reactive calculation: reactiveVar squared
    auto reactiveCalc = reactiveVar * reactiveVar;
    
    // Get initial expression value
    int initialExprValue = reactiveExpr.get();
    
    // Update reactive variable
    reactiveVar.set(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated expression value
    int updatedExprValue = reactiveExpr.get();
    
    // Get squared value after update
    int squaredValue = reactiveCalc.get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        initialExprValue,
        updatedExprValue,
        squaredValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create calculation that tracks recomputation
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&recomputeCount, &var]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return var.get(); // Return current value to make it a valid calculation
    });
    
    // Create batch with the calculation
    auto batch = reaction::batch({calc});
    
    // Check initial batch state
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // Execute the batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // Record recompute count after execution
    int countAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // Close the batch
    batch.close();
    
    // Check final batch state
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        closedInitially,
        closedAfterClose,
        countAfterExecute
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:

        reaction::ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
};

    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto nodeA = std::make_unique<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_unique<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_unique<CacheProbeNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(nodeA.get());
    graph.registerNode(nodeB.get());
    graph.registerNode(nodeC.get());

    // Set up direct observer relationship: A observes B
    nodeB->addOneObserver(nodeA.get());
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship: B observes C
    nodeC->addOneObserver(nodeB.get());

    std::vector<reaction::ObserverNode*> observers;
    graph.getObservers(nodeA.get(), observers);

    // Populate cache data by triggering value changes
    nodeB->valueChanged(true);
    nodeC->valueChanged(true);

    // Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto beforeCleanupStats = graph.getCacheStats();
    size_t graphEntriesBefore = beforeCleanupStats.getGraphEntriesCount();
    size_t hitsBefore = beforeCleanupStats.getHitCount();
    size_t totalRequestsBefore = beforeCleanupStats.getTotalRequests();
    double hitRatioBefore = (totalRequestsBefore > 0) ? 
        static_cast<double>(hitsBefore) / totalRequestsBefore : 0.0;

    // Trigger cache cleanup
    graph.triggerCacheCleanup();

    // Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto afterCleanupStats = graph.getCacheStats();
    size_t graphEntriesAfter = afterCleanupStats.getGraphEntriesCount();
    size_t hitsAfter = afterCleanupStats.getHitCount();
    size_t totalRequestsAfter = afterCleanupStats.getTotalRequests();
    double hitRatioAfter = (totalRequestsAfter > 0) ? 
        static_cast<double>(hitsAfter) / totalRequestsAfter : 0.0;

    // Close all nodes
    nodeA->close();
    nodeB->close();
    nodeC->close();

    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        graphEntriesBefore,
        graphEntriesAfter,
        hitRatioAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    auto& tm = reaction::get_thread_manager();
    tm.reset();
    tm.register_current_thread();
    std::size_t threadCount = tm.get_thread_count();
    
    bool beforeEnable = tm.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    tm.enable_thread_safety();
    bool afterEnable = tm.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    
    tm.reset();
    bool afterReset = tm.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        beforeEnable,
        afterEnable,
        afterReset,
        threadCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    return ThreadGuardState{

        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : value_(value) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() { return value_; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> value_;
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    // Create instances
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);
    
    // Read IDs
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead = first.id();
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead = first.id();
    uint64_t secondId = second.id();
    
    // Update fields to avoid warnings
    first.valueField().get();
    second.valueField().get();
    
    return FieldIdComparison{firstIdFirstRead, firstIdSecondRead, secondId};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitor::disable();
    StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    StackMonitorReport report;
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = StackMonitor::isEnabled();
    
    {
        StackGuard guard(1024 * 64);
        report.guardAllocated = guard.isAllocated();
        report.usageDuringGuard = StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
        
        auto stats = StackMonitor::getStats();
        report.usagePercentage = (static_cast<double>(stats.currentUsage) / stats.limit) * 100.0;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.currentUsage >= stats.limit * 0.9;
    }
    
    report.usageAfterGuard = StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
    StackMonitor::disable();
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = StackMonitor::isEnabled();
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <functional>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Lambda to create large payload with seed-based initialization
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed]() -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + static_cast<int>(i);
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create small SBOResource with initial value
    reaction::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    
    // Create large SBOResource with generated payload
    reaction::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource(createLargePayload());
    
    // Update small resource and capture change status
    SmallPayload newValue{updatedSmall};
    bool updateReportedChange = smallResource.updateValue(newValue);
    
    // Retrieve storage information for small resource
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    bool smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.isUsingSBO();
    std::size_t smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stackUsage();
    
    // Retrieve storage information for large resource
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();
    bool largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.isUsingSBO();
    bool largeInitialized = largeInfo.isInitialized();
    
    return SboStorageStatus{
        updateReportedChange,
        smallUsingSBO,
        smallStackUsage,
        largeInitialized,
        largeUsingSBO
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

}

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    
    for (const auto& code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::describeErrorCode(code));
    }
    
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    
    reaction::ReactionException exception(message, code, file, line, function);
    
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = exception.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = exception.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = exception.getFile();
    info.line = exception.getLine();
    info.function = exception.getFunction();
    info.codeName = exception.getErrorCodeName();
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourcePointerSnapshot {
    int initialPointerValue;
    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;
};

    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    int* ptr1 = resource.getPtr();
    int initialPointerValue = *ptr1;
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    int* ptr2 = resource.getPtr();
    int pointerValueAfterWrite = *ptr2;
    bool pointerStable = (ptr1 == ptr2);
    
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initialPointerValue,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = pointerValueAfterWrite,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with the provided names
    auto var1 = React::var(firstName);
    auto var2 = React::var(secondName);
    auto var3 = React::var(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into a set to order them using React::operator<
    std::set<React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect the ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all collected names are unique
    bool allUnique = orderedNames.size() == 
                    std::set<std::string>(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end()).size();
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Wait, the exact function names might vary. The user mentioned using functions from the `reaction` library, so I should look up the actual methods. But since I don't have access to the library, I'll proceed with common patterns.

The `bindField` function would establish the dependency. Then triggering a change on the field would notify the owner. The `clearDependencies` function would remove the binding. Also, removing the field mapping would dissociate the object from the field node.

After all that, the test should verify that notifications only happen before the cleanup steps, not after.

Putting it all together, the code should create the nodes, register them, set up the binding, test the notification, then clean up and test again. The result struct captures the two boolean checks.

I need to make sure that all steps are followed correctly. Also, the order of operations is important: binding, notification, cleanup, then re-binding and notification.

Potential issues: If the field is not properly unregistered or the binding isn't cleared, the second notification might still trigger. So the cleanup steps must be thorough.

Another thing: After removing the field mapping, trying to bind again might not work, but the code should attempt it anyway. The key is whether the owner gets notified in the second case, which should be false.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // 1. Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    auto owner = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_unique<TrackingFieldNode>("field");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner.get());
    observerGraph.registerNode(field.get());
    
    // 4. Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    // 5. Register field nodes under object ID
    fieldGraph.registerFieldNode(objectId, field.get());
    
    // 6. Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, field.get(), owner.get());
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on field
    field->valueChanged();
    
    // 8. Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // 9. Clear owner notification count
    owner->clear();
    
    // 10. Clear dependencies on owner
    fieldGraph.clearDependencies(owner.get());
    
    // 11. Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.removeFieldMappings(objectId);
    
    // 12. Attempt to bind field again
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, field.get(), owner.get());
    
    // 13. Trigger notification on field again
    field->valueChanged();
    
    // 14. Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // 15. Close nodes
    owner.reset();
    field.reset();
    
    // 16. Return field graph cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto obs = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto node3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(obs);
    graph.registerNode(node1);
    graph.registerNode(node2);
    graph.registerNode(node3);

    graph.attachObserver(node1, obs);

    node1->notify();
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial = obs->notifications();

    graph.detachObserver(node1, obs);
    graph.attachObserver(node2, obs);
    graph.attachObserver(node3, obs);

    node1->notify();
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = obs->notifications();

    node2->notify();
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = obs->notifications();

    node3->notify();
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = obs->notifications();

    node1->close();
    node2->close();
    node3->close();
    obs->close();

    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstNotificationsAfterInitial,
        firstNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        secondNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive boolean variables
    auto a_var = reaction::var(a);
    auto b_var = reaction::var(b);
    auto c_var = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Build logical AND and OR expressions using high-level operators
    auto and_expr = a_var && b_var;
    auto or_expr = a_var || b_var;
    
    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool initialOr = or_expr.get();
    
    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    a_var.set(!a);
    b_var.set(!b);
    
    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool updatedOr = or_expr.get();
    
    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<think>Okay, I need to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function using the `reaction` library. Let me think about the steps required.

Then, after creating the worker thread, we need to wait for it to complete. So using `join()` on the thread object.

After the worker thread completes, check thread safety again.

Finally, return a `ForceRegisterSnapshot` struct containing the three boolean values: safety before, after main, after worker.

Now, putting this all together. Let's outline the code step by step.

1. Get the thread manager instance: `auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();`
2. Reset the state: `tm.reset();`
3. Check safety before: `bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();`
4. Register main thread: `tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();`
5. Check safety after main: `bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();`
6. Create worker thread:

7. Wait for worker: `worker.join();`
8. Check safety after worker: `bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();`
9. Return the snapshot.

Wait, but the worker thread's lambda captures the thread manager reference. Is that safe? Well, since it's a singleton, it's thread-safe. Also, the `forceRegisterCurrentThread()` method should be thread-safe.

But what if the worker thread runs concurrently with the main thread's checks? But in this case, the worker thread is created after the main thread's registration, and we wait for it to complete before checking again. So the sequence should be correct.

So the code would look like:

```cpp
#include <thread>
#include <vector> // for std::thread (if needed)

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([&tm]() {
        tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();
    
    bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{safetyBefore, safetyAfterMain, safetyAfterWorker};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([&tm]() {
        tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();
    
    bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{safetyBefore, safetyAfterMain, safetyAfterWorker};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
